prose.en: When server applications are deployed in their default state, or with an unapproved configuration, it can lead to an insecure operating environment that may allow malicious actors to gain an initial foothold on networks. Many settings exist within server applications enabling them to be configured in an approved secure state to minimise this security risk. As such, ASD and vendors often produce hardening guidance to assist in hardening the configuration of server applications. However, in situations where ASD and vendor hardening guidance conflicts, precedence should be given to implementing the most restrictive guidance.
